performance_overview: Through 5 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) the book has 2 logged 'completed trades', but neither reflects a clean signal-driven round-trip: trade 1 was the 2026-06-04 MPC long (-0.61%, stopped/closed) and trade 2 is the 2026-06-05 corrective BUY-TO-COVER of the unintended -18 MPC short that the 06-04 EOD double-sell created (+0.54%). So the indicator_stats 1W/1L (50%) framing is misleading state-repair noise, not strategy edge — effective clean-signal sample is still ~0 and far too small for any conclusions. Today Bull was +$2.24 (+0.00%) on the day while SPY fell -2.49%, for +2.49% relative alpha earned purely by holding cash through a sharp risk-off session. Since inception cumulative Bull is +0.0067% vs SPY -2.68%, so the strategy is meaningfully ahead of SPY cumulatively — entirely because it has stayed flat/cautious rather than from realized trading gains.
overnight_hold_insights: Still ZERO genuine overnight holds completed across all 5 sessions. The overnight_holds.closed_eod bucket now reads 2 trades / 1W / 50% / avg -0.035%, but that is contaminated: both entries are intraday/EOD closes (the 06-04 MPC long close and today's corrective short-cover), neither a real carry-overnight outcome. No gap-risk data yet. Carry-decision policy is unchanged: apply the config safe-hold gates (profitable, trailing stop active, price > EMA-21, no earnings within 2 days, sentiment not negative, gap-risk cushion). Lesson still standing from MPC: very low-ATR, tight-stop names that don't extend within the session correctly get closed rather than carried.
recent_market_context: 5 sessions 06-01..06-05, all opening calm (VIX ~15.7-16.6, below the 30 suspend). Today (06-05) is the standout: it OPENED calm and trending up (VIX 15.69, SPY EMA9>EMA21, regime cap 10) but the tape turned hard RISK-OFF intraday — SPY closed -2.49%, and by midday internals had broken down across the board (widespread EMA-9 < EMA-21, RSI sagging into the 20s-30s on most watchlist names). Open-session near-misses SNDK/GLW did not firm up; they broke down (SNDK RSI 29.5, GLW RSI 21.6 by midday). Bull took zero entries at both the open and midday checks (none of the 25 watchlist names passed all 7 gates), so flat cash sidestepped the entire drawdown.

If SPY is still below its short-term EMAs Monday, treat it as a down/choppy regime and lean on intraday confirmation; consider a tighter RSI filter and lower position cap until trend re-establishes. No positions tonight so it cannot bite, but the fix remains outstanding. (h) POSITIVE PATTERN: two consecutive sessions (06-04 chop, 06-05 risk-off) where staying flat/cautious in cash beat SPY — selectivity is working; do not force entries into a weak tape just to deploy capital.
